member.name: Andrew Lewin
member.synopsis: Andrew Lewin is the Labour MP for Welwyn Hatfield , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Ukrainian Language: GCSE
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what progress she has made on the introduction of a GCSE in Ukrainian.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions about which languages to offer at GCSE in England are taken by the four independent awarding organisations, AQA, OCR, Pearson Edexcel and WJEC, rather than by central government. We are working with awarding organisations to explore the developm...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Qualifications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has made an assessment of the potential merits of all secondary schools notifying SEND students and their families of the option to study Functional Skills Qualifications instead of GCSEs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is committed to securing an excellent education for every child and your person. We recognise that GCSEs will not be the most appropriate route for every young person. While most pupils at key stage 4 work towards GCSEs or Technical Awards,...

writtenQuestions.heading: State Retirement Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many pension claimants are able to receive the value deferred back through inflated pensions before they pass away.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not know that a person has deferred until they submit a claim for their State Pension. This means it is not possible to identify the total number of people who are deferring at any point in time, the average length of deferral or the n...

writtenQuestions.heading: State Retirement Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, the average period of time for which people choose to defer their state pensions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not know that a person has deferred until they submit a claim for their State Pension. This means it is not possible to identify the total number of people who are deferring at any point in time, the average length of deferral or the n...

writtenQuestions.heading: State Retirement Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many people (a) chose to defer their state pension and (b) claimed an arears payment after 12 months in the most recent 12-month period for which data are available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not know that a person has deferred until they submit a claim for their State Pension. This means it is not possible to identify the total number of people who are deferring at any point in time, the average length of deferral or the n...

writtenQuestions.heading: Colour Vision Deficiency: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what whether she has considered screening all primary school age children for colour blindness.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department knows that effective early identification and intervention is critical in improving the outcomes of children and young people with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND). Whilst the department does not have any current plans to i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Waiting Lists
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to reduce the number of people waiting for NHS treatment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We know how important it is to patients and clinicians that they receive and deliver treatment faster.Our Elective Reform Plan is working. Waiting lists are down over 225,000 since we came to office. We delivered 5.2 million extra appointments in our firs...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Rented Housing: Rents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he plans to implement social rent convergence.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At the Budget on 26 November 2025, the Chancellor restated the government’s commitment to implementing Social Rent convergence. We will announce a decision on how we will do so in January 2026, before the launch of the Social and Affordable Homes Programm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what his planned timetable is to (a) reform the EPC system and (b) introduce a new Home Energy Model in 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is working to reform EPCs so that they support our key objectives of achieving Clean Power by 2030 and accelerating to Net Zero. The government expects to introduce changes to EPCs metrics in 2026. The Home Energy Model (HEM) will replace t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Family Courts: Hertfordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether he has made an assessment of the potential merits of expanding the Pathfinder scheme to Hertfordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Pathfinder model is currently operating in six family court areas across England and Wales. Three further court areas will start the model on 11 November in the Black Country and Shropshire, Worcestershire and Herefordshire and in Stoke-on-Trent and S...

writtenQuestions.heading: Council Housing: Sales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many (a) homes have been purchased through the council homes acquisition programme and (b) former homes bought under right to buy have been purchased back by local authoritie
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on the number of homes purchased specifically through the council homes acquisition programme and on the number of homes sold under right to buy subsequently purchased back by local authorities, is not collected centrally. The number of all acquisiti...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Postgraduate Education: Students
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many postgraduate students are (a) studying and (b) parents of children of nursery age for which the latest data is available..
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Higher Education Statistics Agency (HESA) is responsible for collecting and publishing data on the UK higher education (HE) sector. HESA does not collect data on the parental status of students across all UK HE providers, and so information on the tot...

writtenQuestions.heading: First Time Buyers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many first time buyers there were in (a) 2023, (b) 2024 and (c) 2025; and what proportion of those bought through shared ownership schemes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department does not collect data on the number of first-time buyers.The Office for National Statistics publishes data on first-time buyer mortgage sales by local authority in the UK. They can be found on its website here. Data covers the period between...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: First Time Buyers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what information his Department holds on the average deposit paid by (a) all first time buyers and (b) first time buyers using shared ownership schemes in 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: According to the English Housing Survey, the average (mean) deposit of a first-time buyer in 2023-24 was £55,372 (£32,700 median). Further information can be found in the English Housing Survey statistical publication on gov.uk here.In 2023-24, the mean c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Child Trust Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what is the average value of a redeemed child trust fund since (a) July 2023 and (b) July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The average market value of Child Trust Fund accounts that have matured and have been claimed or transferred to an ISA can be found in the Child Trust Fund tables of the Annual Savings Statistics.https://www.gov.uk/government/statistics/annual-savings-sta...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many schools have entrances which open directly onto a 70mph road in (a) the UK, (b) England and (c) the East of England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold this information. Local traffic authorities are best placed to decide where lower limits will be effective on the roads for which they are responsible, as they are best place to respond with the local information they hold. Ea...

writtenQuestions.heading: Credit Unions: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of a Central Finance Facility for credit unions on the economy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has made clear its strong support for the credit union sector, recognising the value that credit unions bring to their members in local communities across the country in providing savings products and affordable credit. HM Treasury is deliv...

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government: Planning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps her Department is taking to help councils use unspent Section 106 contributions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While there are a variety of entirely legitimate reasons why local planning authorities may be holding unspent developer contributions, including to facilitate the effective delivery of phased development projects, we recognise the need to ensure that the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Ambulance Services: Analgesics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of allowing paramedics to (a) carry and (b) use fentanyl as an analgesic agent.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department remains committed to exploring the extension of medicine responsibilities for non-medical professionals. This will support the aim that patients are cared for, and treated by, the most appropriate healthcare professional to meet their needs...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Concrete
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to her Department's document entitled Volumetric concrete mixers: fact sheet March 2025, published on 18 March 2025, and the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs' guidance entitled
writtenQuestions.answerText: The 18 March publication is not a change to the policy on weights for volumetric concrete mixers. The ten-year temporary exemption will come to an end, as planned, in 2028. The Department assessed the potential environmental impacts when preparing the env...
